channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted is expected to succeed JD Vance in the U.S. Senate, AP sources say
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Republican Ohio Gov. Mike DeWine is expected to select his lieutenant governor, Jon Husted, on Friday to succeed Vice President-elect JD Vance in the U.S. Senate, according to two people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: CNN found guilty in Florida trial of libeling Navy veteran in Afghan story
item.description: A jury in Panama City, Florida on Friday found CNN guilty of defaming a U.S. Navy veteran in a 2021 story about people paid to rescue endangered Afghans following the Taliban takeover of the country.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Driver in Texas migrant smuggling run that led to the deaths of 53 people pleads guilty
item.description: A Texas truck driver is facing jailtime after being charged with the deaths of 53 illegal aliens after transporting them in poor conditions resulting in death.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants one of the agents who covered him after he was shot to lead the Secret Service
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump will choose to lead the U.S. Secret Service one of the agents who covered him after a gunmen opened fire at the candidate during a campaign rally in Pennsylvania. Donald Trump Jr.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Full Israeli Cabinet meets on ceasefire deal after being urged to OK it
item.description: Israel's full Cabinet was meeting Friday evening on the Gaza ceasefire deal that would pause the fighting and release dozens of hostages held by Hamas militants along with Palestinian prisoners held by Israel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden affirms Equal Rights Amendment is part of Constitution
item.description: President Joe Biden on Friday asserted that the Equal Rights Amendment (ERA), which prohibits discrimination on the basis of sex, is part of the Constitution, arguing it had met the criteria to be added as the 28th amendment.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Biden got an Oval Office letter from Trump and may leave one in the desk himself
item.description: Ronald Reagan probably didn't realize he was starting a tradition when he wrote a note congratulating his successor and left it in the Oval Office desk drawer after two terms as president.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Guatemala and Minnesota, holy feast brings migrant families hope and pride amid crackdown fears
item.description: WORTHINGTON, Minn., (AP) — With their sparkling embroideries and colorful sashes flapping in the frigid wind, hundreds of Guatemalan faithful flocked to the Catholic church of this heartland farming town to celebrate their biggest festival yet in honor of the Black Christ of Esquipulas.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Live updates: Noem pledges to eliminate CBP One app; Senate advances Laken Riley Act
item.description: South Dakota Gov. Kristi Noem (R), President-elect Trump's pick to head the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), is appearing for her confirmation hearing before a Senate panel Friday. The purview of DHS involves immigration and federal emergency management — two hot-button topics.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: Biden got an Oval Office letter from Trump and may leave one in the desk himself. It’d be a first
item.description: Every president since Ronald Reagan has left a note for his successor, and President Joe Biden could be the first to write a letter to someone who is both his successor and the predecessor who left a note for him.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Negotiators sign Israel-Hamas hostage deal and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Diddy rape accuser tells her story in NewsNation interview
item.description: Ashley Parham sat down with NewsNation to discuss the harrowing allegations in a civil lawsuit she filed against Combs.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, NewsNation

item.title: Giuliani settles legal fight with former Georgia election workers and agrees to stop defaming them
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Rudy Giuliani reached a deal Thursday that lets the cash-strapped ex-New York City mayor keep his homes and belongings, including prized World Series rings, in exchange for unspecified compensation and a promise to never again speak ill of two former Georgia elections workers who won
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southern California native paid off mortgage day before Eaton Fire consumed home
item.description: While those displaced by the Eaton and Palisades fires are still reeling from the loss of their homes and, in some cases, loved ones, many of them say they’re being victimized yet again by price-gouging landlords.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: As Biden warns of an 'oligarchy,' Trump will be flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: President Joe Biden's pointed warning about the U.S. becoming an “ oligarchy ” of tech billionaires will be illustrated at Donald Trump's inauguration, when the world's three richest men will sit on the dais as Trump is sworn in for a second term.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Drone collides with paraglider, forcing emergency landing
item.description: A paraglider who took off from Torrey Pines State Reserve in California was forced to make an emergency landing when a drone became entwined in his parachute.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: David Lynch, director of 'Twin Peaks,' 'Blue Velvet' and more, dies at 78, family says
item.description: David Lynch, the filmmaker and director behind such movies as “Eraserhead,” “Blue Velvet” and the TV series “Twin Peaks,” has died, his family announced in a message posted to his official Facebook page.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Weelicious founder joins with organization started by KIND Snacks founder to help LA wildfire victims
item.description: Weelicious founder discusses how the Frontline Impact Project is working with her brand and others to get resources to frontline workers fighting the devastating LA wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police detain man dressed in fire gear in Pacific Palisades after reported looting
item.description: Editor’s Note: This article has been updated to include new information provided by the L.A. County Sheriff’s Office. LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Two men, one of whom was dressed as a firefighter, were detained by L.A.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Drone collides with paraglider, forcing emergency landing in California
item.description: A paraglider who took off from Torrey Pines State Reserve in California was forced to make an emergency landing when a drone became entwined in his parachute.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, WGN 9

item.title: TikTok CEO attending Trump inauguration
item.description: TikTok CEO Shou Zi Chew will attend President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration next week, one day after a potential ban of the video-sharing platform could take effect in the U.S., according to a source familiar with the plans.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Fire at home of Florida man missing for 2 years could offer investigators clues
item.description: A recent fire destroyed the home of a Florida man who has been missing for nearly two years, deepening the mystery surrounding his disappearance and reigniting the case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meet the Americans who still take COVID-19 precautions seriously
item.description: Susan Scarbro stares down a bowling lane at the distant pins. She hears a sound that breaks her focus. Was that a cough? Will her mask protect her? COVID-19 remains a very present threat for the 55-year-old. Scarbro has multiple immune disorders, making her vulnerable to infection.
item.dc:creator: DEVNA BOSE and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Louisiana governor relocating homeless people ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: Louisiana authorities are clearing homeless encampments around the Caesars Superdome in New Orleans ahead of next month's Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: He left his LA-area home to cover the wildfires. But the flames were barreling toward his front door
item.description: ALTADENA, Calif. (AP) — The warnings began on Saturday, Jan. 4. Our neighbor, a volunteer at the local sheriff's station, texted that we should “batten down the hatches” for a “big windstorm." From there, things moved fast.
item.dc:creator: RYAN PEARSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US government sues Southwest Airlines, fines Frontier for chronically delayed flights
item.description: “Airlines have a legal obligation to ensure that their flight schedules provide travelers with realistic departure and arrival times," Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 2 found dead in compartment of JetBlue plane at Florida airport
item.description: The two were found dead during a routine post-flight maintenance inspection on Monday, according to JetBlue.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden warns of dangers of 'oligarchy' of ultra-rich, 'tech-industrial complex' running country
item.description: President Joe Biden will deliver his farewell address to the nation on Wednesday, days before President-elect Donald Trump returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida grandmother who left infant in hot car found not guilty of manslaughter
item.description: A Florida grandmother was acquitted of manslaughter in the death of her 7-month-old granddaughter, but was convicted on a lesser charge.
item.dc:creator: Staci DaSilva, KRON4

item.title: Southwest pilot arrested for suspected DUI at Georgia airport
item.description: A Southwest flight was delayed for five hours Wednesday morning due to a pilot being arrested and charged with DUI, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Whoopi Goldberg backs Carrie Underwood inauguration performance
item.description: The comments about Underwood’s inauguration performance are notable coming from Goldberg, who is commonly a harsh critic of Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Disabled Los Angeles fire victim escaped flames in wheelchair
item.description: Galen Buckwalter, who is paralyzed from the waist down, said he watched as the glow of the wildfire raced down the San Gabriel Mountains toward their home in the foothill community of Sierra Madre.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US in plea talks with Mexican cartel kingpin Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. prosecutors said Wednesday that they're discussing a potential plea deal with Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada, the long-elusive Mexican drug lord who was arrested last summer and whose son could testify against him if he goes to trial. Assistant U.S.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FAFSA forms to make October release after two-year struggle
item.description: The Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) forms will be released on Oct. 1 this year, the Department of Education announced on Wednesday, hitting the normal deadline after two chaotic years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: A retro blue VW van miraculously survives deadly Los Angeles fire
item.description: Preston Martin figured the retro blue Volkswagen van he slept in for a year during college was a goner, given that he parked it in a Malibu neighborhood just before the Palisades fire ripped through, reducing homes and cars to rubble and charred metal.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Capital One sued by US watchdog, accused of cheating customers out of $2 billion
item.description: A U.S. watchdog is suing Capital One for allegedly misleading consumers about its offerings for high-interest savings accounts — and “cheating" customers out of more than $2 billion in lost interest payments as a result.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hanging out at Starbucks? It will cost you as company reverses its open-door policy
item.description: If you want to hang out or use the restroom at Starbucks, you’re going to have to buy something. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores. Starbucks on Monday said it was reversing a policy that invited everyone into its stores.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
